metadata.title: Build the Grading Tool Phase 1 with Claude: Survey Before You Grade

beats.narration_text: The first build step is the safest one — and the one everyone skips. It proves your tool can't modify a submission file before it ever touches one.

Phase 1 of the grading tool is the survey pass: list submissions, report format and size, and exit without modifying any file. The dry-run flag is the contractual form of the read-only guarantee. Building Phase 1 before Phase 2 means the first thing you know about your grading tool is that it cannot accidentally overwrite student work.
Build survey.py: list submissions, handle py, ipynb, and zip formats, print a table, read-only, with a dry-run flag. Standard library only.

props.command: claude "Implement survey.py that lists all student submissions in a directory I specify. Handle .py, .ipynb, and .zip formats. For zip files, list the contained Python files. Print a table: submission ID, format, file size. Read-only — no file modifications. Add a --dry-run flag that confirms what it would do. Python 3.11, standard library only."

beats.narration_text: Read it before trusting it. Pathlib iteration, zipfile inspection, formatted table, argparse for dry-run, sys.exit zero with no file writes.

props.code:
import argparse, sys, zipfile
from pathlib import Path

def survey(directory, dry_run=False):
    d = Path(directory)
    rows = []
    for f in sorted(d.iterdir()):
        if f.suffix == '.zip':
            with zipfile.ZipFile(f) as z:
                py = [n for n in z.namelist()
                      if n.endswith('.py')]
            rows.append((f.stem,'zip',
                         f'{f.stat().st_size/1024:.1f}KB',
                         f'{len(py)} .py inside'))
        elif f.suffix in ('.py','.ipynb'):
            rows.append((f.stem, f.suffix[1:],
                         f'{f.stat().st_size/1024:.1f}KB', ''))
    # print table — no writes
    fmt = '{:<20} {:<8} {:<10} {}'
    print(fmt.format('ID','Format','Size','Notes'))
    for r in rows: print(fmt.format(*r))
    sys.exit(0)   # read-only guarantee

beats.narration_text: survey.py generated: standard library only, verified. Run on 5 test submissions: table shows all formats. Timestamp verification: all 5 file mtimes unchanged after run. Read-only guarantee holds.
Add a format filter flag: dash-dash format py to list only Python submissions.

props.command: claude "Add --format filter flag to survey.py: --format py lists only Python submissions. Preserve read-only constraint. Standard library only."

beats.narration_text: Extended survey.py: dash-dash format py lists only Python submissions. Read-only constraint verified post-extension: no file writes, no timestamp changes. The extension added a feature without touching the constraint — which is what the AGENTS.md never-rules protect.

Phase 1 is the conducting discipline before stakes are real. The smallest possible step that can fail safely: list, report, exit. The dry-run flag makes the read-only guarantee testable before the tool runs on real submissions. Every subsequent phase builds on the guarantee Phase 1 established.
Next: generate-evaluate-select — why one Claude response is a default, not a choice.
